mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it suitable for production environments requiring reliable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the flexibility and scalability of storage solutions offered.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Rook Ceph Operator.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Ceph Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook Ceph Operator in the 'rook-ceph' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-ceph

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook Ceph.
> Installs the Rook Ceph Operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create the Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

```

> Lists all pods in the rook-ceph namespace to verify their status.
> Executes a command in the Rook Ceph tools pod to check the Ceph cluster status.
commands: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph

kubectl delete namespace rook-ceph

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade the Rook Ceph Operator, including backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd pool ls --format json > ceph-pools-backup.json

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

```

> Backs up the current Ceph pools configuration.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd pool ls --format json > ceph-pools-backup.json
helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create pool'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the Ceph cluster.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation and review the cluster configuration for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues within the Ceph cluster that need to be addressed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph health detail' to get more information on the warnings.

troubleshooting.title: Rook operator pod is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient permissions or RBAC issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the necessary RBAC roles and bindings are correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for CephFS and RBD snapshots, added new monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy configurations for Ceph clusters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, ensure to update your Ceph cluster CRD definitions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod' showing all pods in the 'Running' state, and 'ceph status' should report 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.3
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
